tasks.spec: You are a read-only scout in a multi-agent review swarm for mcp-local-rag (a local RAG server exposing MCP + CLI: semantic search with keyword boost over a LanceDB vector store; it serves the per-domain vector corpora used by the Meridian knowledge system). Boundary: the repo at '/Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag' is source material ONLY — never modify, create, delete, format, install, or commit anything inside it. You own exactly one output file, in your current task directory: ./report.md. Your surface is the LanceDB vector-store layer: src/vectordb/ (entry points: vectordb.ts, search-filters.ts, optimize-lock.ts, and the module index). Review only that surface and its direct dependencies; if you notice an issue elsewhere, note it under Assumptions rather than expanding scope. Review dimensions: correctness of vector search and filter construction; concurrency and locking around index optimization (optimize-lock); resource lifecycle (are connections/tables opened and always closed, even on error?); behavior on an empty, missing, or corrupt index; and data-integrity risks that could corrupt or silently drop rows in a Meridian corpus. HOW TO RUN: inspect with read-only commands only, always using the absolute repo path, e.g. `rg <pattern> /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/src/vectordb`, `sed -n '1,80p' /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/src/vectordb/optimize-lock.ts`, `git -C /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag log --oneline -5 -- src/vectordb`. Do NOT run the test suite, build, or install anything. OUTPUT CONTRACT for ./report.md: start with '# Review Report'. Include '## Summary' (at most 3 bullets), '## Findings', '## Clean', and '## Assumptions'. Each finding must use exactly: '### Finding: <neutral summary>', then 'Evidence:', 'Impact:', 'Fix:', 'Priority: P0|P1|P2|P3', and 'Confidence: high|medium|low'. Evidence must cite file:line (e.g. src/vectordb/optimize-lock.ts:42) and quote only the needed words. If there are no findings, write 'No findings for this surface.' under Findings and still fill Clean by dimension. Hard rules: no invented facts, mark every assumption, never modify anything outside your task directory, keep the report under 1200 words.
tasks.verified: report.md follows the review contract (titled sections; every finding carries file:line evidence, impact, fix, P0-P3 priority, and confidence), proving a structured, evidence-anchored review of the LanceDB vectordb layer rather than an unverifiable summary.

tasks.spec: You are a read-only scout in a multi-agent review swarm for mcp-local-rag (a local RAG server exposing MCP + CLI: semantic search with keyword boost over a LanceDB vector store; it serves the per-domain vector corpora used by the Meridian knowledge system). Boundary: the repo at '/Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag' is source material ONLY — never modify, create, delete, format, install, or commit anything inside it. You own exactly one output file, in your current task directory: ./report.md. Your surface is the ingestion front half: src/chunker/ (semantic-chunker.ts, sentence-splitter.ts) and src/embedder/ (the embedder and its lazy initialization) — how documents are split into chunks by embedding similarity and how the embedding model is loaded and invoked. Review only that surface and its direct dependencies; note anything outside it under Assumptions. Review dimensions: chunk-boundary correctness; edge cases (empty/whitespace-only, very large, non-English/Unicode, and code-block-heavy inputs); lazy-initialization race conditions or double-initialization of the embedder; error handling when embedding fails mid-batch; and memory/throughput on large documents. HOW TO RUN: read-only commands only, always using the absolute repo path, e.g. `rg <pattern> /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/src/chunker /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/src/embedder`, `sed -n '1,120p' /Users/usjoh/Projects/mcp-local-rag/src/chunker/semantic-chunker.ts`. Do NOT run the test suite, build, or install anything. OUTPUT CONTRACT for ./report.md: start with '# Review Report'. Each finding must use exactly: '### Finding: <neutral summary>', then 'Evidence:', 'Impact:', 'Fix:', 'Priority: P0|P1|P2|P3', and 'Confidence: high|medium|low'. Evidence must cite file:line and quote only the needed words. If there are no findings, write 'No findings for this surface.' under Findings and still fill Clean by dimension. Your surface is the document-parsing layer: src/parser/ (html-parser, parsePdf/parsePdfPages, title-extractor, pdf-filter) and src/pdf-visual/ (detector, renderer, captioner, orchestrator) — turning HTML and PDF, including visual/image content, into text.
